description_long: This object is a single half of an avocado, with its interior exposed and the pit removed. The inner flesh is a pale green, smooth but slightly uneven, with a central indentation where the pit once was. The outer skin is rough, thick, and mottled with brown, yellow, and green hues, showing a rugged, bumpy texture typical of Hass avocados. The cut edge is irregular, suggesting it was sliced open by hand or with a knife. The avocado's shape is roughly conical, tapering to a rounded point at one end and wider at the open cut. There are visible blemishes and discolorations on the skin, indicating natural ripening and handling.
description: A single half of an avocado with pale green flesh and a rough, mottled brown-green skin, showing the pit cavity and irregular cut edge.
description_view_0: The open, cut side of the avocado is visible, showing the pale green flesh and central pit cavity.
description_view_1: The avocado is seen from the side, highlighting the thick, rough, and mottled skin with a conical shape.
description_view_2: The view focuses on the rounded, pointed end of the avocado, emphasizing the bumpy, brownish skin.
description_view_3: A side angle showing both the rough skin and a glimpse of the green interior near the cut edge.
attribute_skin_blemishes: mottled discoloration, rough patches, minor scars
